preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the lateral approximant [l] is a form of liquidization or nasal-to-liquid shift. It is frequently seen in dialectal variation or as a result of dissimilation when multiple nasals occur in close proximity.

examples.note: In Yoruba and related Niger-Congo languages, /n/ and /l/ are often allophones or alternate freely in certain environments; e.g., some speakers produce 'inu' (inside) as 'ilu' dialectally.
Documented in specific dialectal variations and loanword adaptations.
